steps.title: Translate the question
steps.content: The predicted $y$-value is the height of the line of best fit at the same $x$-value as the data point. A point has an actual $y$-value greater than the predicted value exactly when the point is visually above the line.

steps.title: Count by residual sign
steps.content: Scan from left to right and mark each point as above, on, or below the line. Only points strictly above the line count; points on the line would have actual and predicted values equal, so they do not count.

steps.title: Match the count
steps.content: The scatterplot has $6$ points above the line of best fit and $4$ points at or below it. The number of data points with actual $y$-values greater than predicted $y$-values is $6$, which matches choice C.
